name: edge
context: Build Vercel Edge Functions and Middleware for low-latency compute at the CDN edge
info: Vercel Edge Functions and Middleware run on V8 isolates at CDN nodes worldwide, providing sub-millisecond cold starts and low-latency request processing. Edge Middleware intercepts every request before it reaches the origin, enabling authentication checks, A/B testing, geolocation-based routing, and request rewriting without a round-trip to the origin server. Edge Functions are standalone compute endpoints using the Web standard Request/Response API. The edge runtime is a subset of Node.js — it supports fetch, crypto, TextEncoder, URL, and other Web APIs but does not support Node.js-specific modules like fs, net, or child_process. Use edge for latency-critical paths; use [[vercel/serverless]] for full Node.js API compatibility.

sections.title: Edge functions
sections.body: Edge functions are API endpoints that run on the edge runtime. Set `export const runtime = 'edge'` in Next.js route handlers or `export const config = { runtime: 'edge' }` in standalone `api/` files. Edge functions use the Web standard Request/Response API. They execute at the nearest CDN node to the user, providing low-latency responses.

sections.title: Edge middleware
sections.body: Middleware intercepts requests before they reach the origin. Define a `middleware.ts` file at the project root. The function receives a `NextRequest` and can rewrite URLs, redirect, set headers, or return responses directly. Middleware runs on every request matching the configured `matcher` patterns. Use for auth checks, A/B testing, feature flags, and geolocation routing. See [[vercel/deploy]] for deploying middleware with the project.

sections.title: Path matchers
sections.body: The `config.matcher` array controls which URL paths trigger middleware execution. Use Next.js path patterns with `:path*` for catch-all segments. Exclude static assets and internal paths (`_next`) with negative lookaheads. Without a matcher, middleware runs on every request. Keep matchers specific to avoid unnecessary middleware execution on static asset requests.

sections.title: Geolocation data
sections.body: Edge functions and middleware receive geolocation data via `request.geo` (or `request.headers` with `x-vercel-ip-city`, `x-vercel-ip-country`, etc.). Use geolocation for country-based redirects, regional content, compliance (GDPR), and localization. Geolocation is derived from the client IP and is available only at the edge — [[vercel/serverless]] functions receive forwarded headers instead.

sections.title: Edge runtime limitations
sections.body: The edge runtime does not support Node.js built-in modules (`fs`, `net`, `child_process`, `crypto` Node.js API). Use Web standard equivalents (`fetch`, `crypto.subtle`, `TextEncoder`). Maximum execution time is 30 seconds. Maximum response body is 4MB. No access to the filesystem. Database access requires HTTP-based clients (e.g., Prisma Edge, PlanetScale serverless driver). For full Node.js support, use [[vercel/serverless]].
